family_context.preferred_cover_geography: Georgia and Germany, ideally worldwide excluding the US

worldwide_ex_us_status_legend.explicitly_indicated: A reviewed public source explicitly described a worldwide excluding U.S. (or equivalent outside-U.S.) coverage area/plan, or used materially equivalent wording such as Area 2 worldwide excluding the USA.
worldwide_ex_us_status_legend.indirectly_indicated: A reviewed public source gave strong source-backed signals of a non-U.S. global configuration, such as an include-USA toggle, U.S. add-on, or outside-U.S. benefit wording, but did not explicitly state a worldwide-excluding-U.S. area of cover for the exact plan wording captured.
worldwide_ex_us_status_legend.worldwide_positioned_but_ex_us_not_verif: A reviewed public source positioned the plan as worldwide/global/international, but the captured evidence did not explicitly confirm a worldwide-excluding-U.S. configuration.
worldwide_ex_us_status_legend.not_verified_from_reviewed_sources: The reviewed public sources in this run did not verify a worldwide/global geography detail sufficient to infer a worldwide-excluding-U.S. configuration.

notes: This is a discovery-only geography screening ledger, not a recommendation or eligibility determination.
worldwide_ex_us_indicated=true means the reviewed public source materially indicated a non-U.S. global configuration; it does not guarantee acceptance for a German family resident in Georgia.
Candidates without a positive indication remain in-scope when they still show credible international-family positioning and need deeper quote or policy-document review.
